package agent

import (
	"context"
	"fmt"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"testing"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/ddxroot"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestResolveAttemptBackend_DefaultsToWorktree(t *testing.T) {
	rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForBead(config.TestBeadConfigOpts{}).Resolve(config.CLIOverrides{})
	backend, err := ResolveAttemptBackend(rcfg)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, AttemptBackendWorktree, backend.Name())
}

func TestResolveAttemptBackend_DockerCloneFromOverride(t *testing.T) {
	rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForBead(config.TestBeadConfigOpts{}).Resolve(config.CLIOverrides{
		AttemptBackend: AttemptBackendDockerClone,
	})
	backend, err := ResolveAttemptBackend(rcfg)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, AttemptBackendDockerClone, backend.Name())
}

func TestResolveAttemptBackend_RejectsUnknownBackend(t *testing.T) {
	rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForBead(config.TestBeadConfigOpts{}).Resolve(config.CLIOverrides{
		AttemptBackend: "bogus",
	})
	_, err := ResolveAttemptBackend(rcfg)
	require.Error(t, err)
	require.Contains(t, err.Error(), "unknown attempt backend")
}

func TestExecuteBeadWithConfig_LocalCloneBackendImportsResult(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot, _ := newScriptHarnessRepo(t, 1)
	const beadID = "ddx-int-0001"

	directivePath := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directive.txt")
	writeDirectiveFile(t, directivePath, []string{
		"append-line output.txt from local clone backend",
		"commit chore: local clone backend output",
	})

	cfg := config.NewTestConfigForBead(config.TestBeadConfigOpts{Model: directivePath})
	rcfg := cfg.Resolve(config.CLIOverrides{Harness: "script"})
	res, err := ExecuteBeadWithConfig(context.Background(), projectRoot, beadID, rcfg, ExecuteBeadRuntime{
		AgentRunner:    NewRunner(Config{}),
		AttemptBackend: LocalCloneAttemptBackend{},
	}, &RealGitOps{})
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.NotNil(t, res)
	require.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, res.Status)
	require.NotEmpty(t, res.ResultRev)
	require.NotEqual(t, res.BaseRev, res.ResultRev)

	out, catErr := runGitIntegOutput(projectRoot, "cat-file", "-e", res.ResultRev+"^{commit}")
	require.NoError(t, catErr, out)

	landing, landErr := LandBeadResult(projectRoot, res, &RealOrchestratorGitOps{}, BeadLandingOptions{
		LandingAdvancer: func(r *ExecuteBeadResult) (*LandResult, error) {
			return Land(projectRoot, BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, r), RealLandingGitOps{})
		},
	})
	require.NoError(t, landErr)
	require.Equal(t, "merged", landing.Outcome)

	showOut, showErr := runGitIntegOutput(projectRoot, "show", "HEAD:output.txt")
	require.NoError(t, showErr, showOut)
	require.Contains(t, showOut, "from local clone backend")
}

func TestDockerRunArgs_AppliesResourceLimitsAndMounts(t *testing.T) {
	ws := &AttemptWorkspace{
		ProjectRoot: "/repo/project",
		WorkDir:     "/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-clone-ddx-1-attempt",
		BeadID:      "ddx-1",
		AttemptID:   "20260518T100000-deadbeef",
		DockerRun:   "/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-runtime-ddx-1-attempt",
		DockerHome:  "/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-runtime-ddx-1-attempt/home",
	}
	args := dockerRunArgs(&config.ExecutionsDockerConfig{
		Memory:     "8g",
		MemorySwap: "8g",
		CPUs:       "4",
		PidsLimit:  1024,
		TmpfsSize:  "2g",
		Network:    "none",
	}, ws, "/usr/bin/ddx", "runner:latest", []dockerToolMount{{Name: "codex", Path: "/usr/bin/codex"}})

	require.Contains(t, args, "--rm")
	require.Contains(t, args, "--init")
	require.Contains(t, args, "--memory")
	require.Contains(t, args, "8g")
	require.Contains(t, args, "--memory-swap")
	require.Contains(t, args, "--cpus")
	require.Contains(t, args, "--pids-limit")
	require.Contains(t, args, "--network")
	require.Contains(t, args, "/tmp:rw,nosuid,nodev,size=2g,mode=1777")
	require.Contains(t, args, "type=bind,src=/usr/bin/ddx,dst=/usr/local/bin/ddx,readonly")
	require.Contains(t, args, "PATH=/usr/local/go/bin:/opt/go/bin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin")
	require.Contains(t, args, "HOME=/ddx-runtime/home")
	require.Contains(t, args, "GOCACHE=/work/.gocache")
	require.Contains(t, args, "GOTMPDIR=/ddx-runtime/go-tmp")
	require.NotContains(t, args, "GOMODCACHE=/ddx-runtime/go/pkg/mod")
	require.NotContains(t, args, "GOCACHE=/ddx-runtime/go-build-cache")
	require.Contains(t, args, "type=bind,src=/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-runtime-ddx-1-attempt,dst=/ddx-runtime")
	require.Contains(t, args, "type=bind,src=/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-runtime-ddx-1-attempt/work-gocache,dst=/work/.gocache")
	require.Contains(t, args, "type=bind,src=/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-runtime-ddx-1-attempt/work-tmp,dst=/work/.tmp")
	require.Contains(t, args, "type=bind,src=/usr/bin/codex,dst=/usr/local/bin/codex,readonly")
	require.Equal(t, "runner:latest", args[len(args)-1])
}

func TestLocalCloneAttemptBackendExcludesTransientMountDirs(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot, baseRev := newScriptHarnessRepo(t, 1)
	ws, err := (LocalCloneAttemptBackend{}).Prepare(context.Background(), AttemptBackendPrepareRequest{
		ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
		BeadID:      "ddx-int-0001",
		AttemptID:   "20260518T100000-deadbeef",
		BaseRev:     baseRev,
	})
	require.NoError(t, err)
	t.Cleanup(func() {
		_ = (LocalCloneAttemptBackend{}).Cleanup(context.Background(), ws)
	})

	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(ws.WorkDir, ".gocache"), 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(ws.WorkDir, ".tmp"), 0o755))

	excludePath, err := runGitIntegOutput(ws.WorkDir, "rev-parse", "--git-path", "info/exclude")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	if !filepath.IsAbs(excludePath) {
		excludePath = filepath.Join(ws.WorkDir, excludePath)
	}
	excludeRaw, err := os.ReadFile(excludePath)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Contains(t, string(excludeRaw), "/.gocache/")
	require.Contains(t, string(excludeRaw), "/.tmp/")

	out, err := runGitIntegOutput(ws.WorkDir, "check-ignore", "-v", ".gocache", ".tmp")
	require.NoError(t, err, out)
	require.Contains(t, out, "/.gocache/")
	require.Contains(t, out, "/.tmp/")

	status, err := runGitIntegOutput(ws.WorkDir, "status", "--short")
	require.NoError(t, err, status)
	require.NotContains(t, status, ".gocache")
	require.NotContains(t, status, ".tmp")
}

func TestPrepareDockerAttemptHomeCopiesMinimalAuth(t *testing.T) {
	hostHome := t.TempDir()
	t.Setenv("HOME", hostHome)
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(hostHome, ".codex"), 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(hostHome, ".claude"), 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(hostHome, ".codex", "auth.json"), []byte(`{"token":"test"}`), 0o600))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(hostHome, ".codex", "config.toml"), []byte("model = 'test'\n"), 0o600))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(hostHome, ".codex", "logs_2.sqlite"), []byte("large runtime state"), 0o600))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(hostHome, ".claude", ".credentials.json"), []byte(`{"credential":"test"}`), 0o600))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(hostHome, ".claude", "history.jsonl"), []byte("runtime history"), 0o600))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(hostHome, ".claude.json"), []byte(`{"projects":{}}`), 0o600))
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(hostHome, ".local", "state", "fizeau"), 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(hostHome, ".local", "state", "fizeau", "claude-quota.json"), []byte(`{"five_hour_remaining":96}`), 0o600))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(hostHome, ".local", "state", "fizeau", "codex-quota.json"), []byte(`{"remaining":50}`), 0o600))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(hostHome, ".local", "state", "fizeau", "gemini-quota.json"), []byte(`{"remaining":50}`), 0o600))

	attemptHome := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "attempt-home")
	require.NoError(t, prepareDockerAttemptHome(attemptHome))

	require.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(attemptHome, ".codex", "auth.json"))
	require.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(attemptHome, ".codex", "config.toml"))
	require.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(attemptHome, ".claude", ".credentials.json"))
	require.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(attemptHome, ".claude.json"))
	require.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(attemptHome, ".local", "state", "fizeau", "claude-quota.json"))
	require.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(attemptHome, ".local", "state", "fizeau", "codex-quota.json"))
	require.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(attemptHome, ".local", "state", "fizeau", "gemini-quota.json"))
	require.NoFileExists(t, filepath.Join(attemptHome, ".codex", "logs_2.sqlite"))
	require.NoFileExists(t, filepath.Join(attemptHome, ".claude", "history.jsonl"))
}

func TestPrepareDockerAttemptRuntimeCreatesCacheRoots(t *testing.T) {
	runDir := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "attempt-runtime")
	require.NoError(t, prepareDockerAttemptRuntime(runDir))

	for _, dir := range []string{
		"cache",
		filepath.Join("go", "pkg", "mod"),
		"go-build-cache",
		"go-tmp",
		"tmp",
		"work-gocache",
		"work-tmp",
	} {
		require.DirExists(t, filepath.Join(runDir, dir))
	}
}

func TestDockerProjectDockerfileAutodetectsProjectLayer(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot := t.TempDir()
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxroot.InTree(projectRoot), 0o755))
	dockerfile := ddxroot.InTree(projectRoot, "attempt-runner.Dockerfile")
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(dockerfile, []byte("FROM scratch\n"), 0o644))

	got, ok, err := dockerProjectDockerfile(projectRoot, nil)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.True(t, ok)
	require.Equal(t, dockerfile, got)
}

func TestDockerProjectDockerfileRejectsEscapes(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot := t.TempDir()
	_, _, err := dockerProjectDockerfile(projectRoot, &config.ExecutionsDockerConfig{
		ProjectDockerfile: "../Dockerfile",
	})
	require.Error(t, err)

data.text: I’ve got the test helper layer now. The next gap is the exact on-disk naming for local-clone attempts and preserve refs, because the acceptance checks need filesystem-level assertions, not just stdout. I’m reading the attempt-backend path helpers and the current `ddx try` integration tests for those names.

// TestTry_HappyPath_ClaimsAndExecutes verifies the core AC: given a ready bead
// and a stub executor that returns success, the command claims the bead,
// invokes the executor, and exits zero.
func TestTry_HappyPath_ClaimsAndExecutes(t *testing.T) {
	env := NewTestEnvironment(t)
	store := bead.NewStore(env.Dir + "/.ddx")
	require.NoError(t, store.Init())
	require.NoError(t, store.Create(&bead.Bead{
		ID:    "happy-bead-001",
		Title: "Happy path bead",
	}))

	var executorCalled bool
	stubExecutor := agent.ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
		executorCalled = true
		assert.Equal(t, "happy-bead-001", beadID, "executor must receive the targeted bead ID")
		return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
			BeadID:    beadID,
			Status:    agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
			ResultRev: "deadbeef01234567", // non-empty so ClosureGate passes
		}, nil
	})

	factory := NewCommandFactory(env.Dir)
	factory.AgentRunnerOverride = &tryHookRunnerStub{t: t}
	factory.tryExecutorOverride = stubExecutor
	root := factory.NewRootCommand()

	out, err := executeCommand(root, "try", "happy-bead-001", "--no-review", "--no-review-i-know-what-im-doing")
	require.NoError(t, err, "ddx try with a successful stub executor must exit zero: %s", out)
	assert.True(t, executorCalled, "executor must have been called")

	// Verify the bead was claimed (status should be closed after success).
	b, storeErr := store.Get("happy-bead-001")
	require.NoError(t, storeErr)
	assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusClosed, b.Status, "bead must be closed after successful execution")
}

func TestTry_ForceClaimBypassesCooldown(t *testing.T) {
	env := NewTestEnvironment(t)
	store := bead.NewStore(env.Dir + "/.ddx")
	require.NoError(t, store.Init())
	require.NoError(t, store.Create(&bead.Bead{
		ID:    "cooldown-bead-001",
		Title: "Cooldown bead",
	}))
	require.NoError(t, store.SetExecutionCooldown(
		"cooldown-bead-001",
		time.Now().UTC().Add(6*time.Hour),
		agent.ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges,
		"retry later",
		"base-rev-1",
	))

	var calls int
	factory := NewCommandFactory(env.Dir)
	factory.AgentRunnerOverride = &tryHookRunnerStub{t: t}
	factory.tryExecutorOverride = agent.ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
		calls++
		return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
			BeadID:    beadID,
			Status:    agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
			ResultRev: "forced-success-rev",
		}, nil
	})

	out, err := executeCommand(
		factory.NewRootCommand(),
		"try", "cooldown-bead-001",
		"--no-review",
		"--no-review-i-know-what-im-doing",
	)
	require.Error(t, err, "ddx try without --force-claim must respect retry cooldown")
	assert.Contains(t, out, "retry cooldown")
	assert.Equal(t, 0, calls, "executor must not run without --force-claim")

	out, err = executeCommand(
		factory.NewRootCommand(),
		"try", "cooldown-bead-001",
		"--force-claim",
		"--reason", "operator unblock",
		"--no-review",
		"--no-review-i-know-what-im-doing",
	)
	require.NoError(t, err, "ddx try --force-claim must execute cooled bead: %s", out)
	assert.Equal(t, 1, calls, "executor must run once when cooldown is explicitly bypassed")

	got, getErr := store.Get("cooldown-bead-001")
	require.NoError(t, getErr)
	assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusClosed, got.Status)
}

data.text: I’ve got the safe directive shape now: a single shell `run` step that mutates only when the subprocess is inside the local-clone attempt worktree, and becomes a no-op if any hook reuses it from the project root. I’m adding the three concurrency tests plus the small bundle/env helpers in a new integration file so the existing lock-contention proof stays untouched.

The first pass is in place, but I spotted one likely compile/runtime issue before running tests: the script-harness directive must be a single directive line, not a multiline shell script. I’m flattening that now and then I’ll run the targeted integration slice to see what the real `ddx try` behavior looks like under the harness.

package integration

import (
	"context"
	"errors"
	"fmt"
	"os"
	"os/exec"
	"path/filepath"
	"sort"
	"strings"
	"sync"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/ddxroot"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/testutils"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

type trySubprocessResult struct {
	beadID   string
	output   string
	err      error
	exitCode int
}

func TestIntegration_ConcurrentTryDistinctBeads_LocalClone(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot, bin, execRoot, env, beadIDs := setupConcurrentTryFixture(t, 8)
	directivePath := writeTryDirective(t, "success")

	results := runConcurrentTrySubprocesses(t, bin, projectRoot, env, beadIDs[:4], directivePath, false)
	assertAllTrySubprocessesFinished(t, results, 0)

	store := bead.NewStore(ddxroot.JoinProject(projectRoot))
	for _, beadID := range beadIDs[:4] {
		b, err := store.Get(context.Background(), beadID)
		require.NoError(t, err)
		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusClosed, b.Status, "bead %s must close after a successful try", beadID)
	}

	bundles := completeExecutionBundles(t, projectRoot)
	require.Len(t, bundles, 4, "each successful try must publish one execution bundle")
	require.Len(t, uniqueStrings(bundles), 4, "execution bundle attempt ids must be unique")
	assertNoRemainingCloneDirs(t, execRoot)
}

func TestIntegration_ConcurrentTrySameBead_OneClaimWins(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot, bin, execRoot, env, beadIDs := setupConcurrentTryFixture(t, 1)
	directivePath := writeTryDirective(t, "slow-success")
	beadID := beadIDs[0]

	results := runConcurrentTrySubprocesses(t, bin, projectRoot, env, []string{beadID, beadID, beadID}, directivePath, false)

	winners := 0
	losers := 0
	for _, res := range results {
		if res.err == nil {
			winners++
			assert.Contains(t, res.output, "status: success", "winner must report a successful try")
			assert.Contains(t, res.output, "result_rev:", "winner must report a result revision")
			continue
		}
		losers++
		assertTryClaimLossOutput(t, res)
	}
	require.Equal(t, 1, winners, "exactly one subprocess must win the claim")
	require.Equal(t, 2, losers, "the remaining subprocesses must lose the claim race")

	store := bead.NewStore(ddxroot.JoinProject(projectRoot))
	b, err := store.Get(context.Background(), beadID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusClosed, b.Status, "the winning subprocess must close the bead")

	bundles := completeExecutionBundles(t, projectRoot)
	require.Len(t, bundles, 1, "only the winning attempt should publish a terminal bundle")
	assertNoRemainingCloneDirs(t, execRoot)
}

func TestIntegration_ConcurrentTryPreserveRefsUnique(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot, bin, execRoot, env, beadIDs := setupConcurrentTryFixture(t, 3)
	directivePath := writeTryDirective(t, "success")

	waitForSecondBoundary()
	results := runConcurrentTrySubprocesses(t, bin, projectRoot, env, beadIDs, directivePath, true)

	preserveRefs := make([]string, 0, len(results))
	attemptPrefixes := make([]string, 0, len(results))
	for _, res := range results {
		require.NoError(t, res.err, "preserved try should exit successfully as a preserved attempt")
		preserveRef := extractValue(res.output, "preserve_ref: ")
		require.NotEmpty(t, preserveRef, "preserved try must report a preserve ref")
		preserveRefs = append(preserveRefs, preserveRef)

		attemptID := attemptIDFromPreserveRef(preserveRef)
		require.NotEmpty(t, attemptID, "preserve ref must encode an attempt id")
		attemptPrefixes = append(attemptPrefixes, attemptTimestampPrefix(attemptID))
	}

	assert.Len(t, uniqueStrings(preserveRefs), len(preserveRefs), "preserve refs must be unique")
	assert.Len(t, uniqueStrings(attemptPrefixes), 1, "all attempts should share the same second-level timestamp prefix")

	refsOut := runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "for-each-ref", "--format=%(refname)", "refs/ddx/iterations")
	for _, ref := range preserveRefs {
		assert.Contains(t, refsOut, ref, "git must contain the preserved iteration ref")
	}

	bundles := completeExecutionBundles(t, projectRoot)
	require.Len(t, bundles, 3, "each preserved try must still publish an execution bundle")
	assertNoRemainingCloneDirs(t, execRoot)
}

func setupConcurrentTryFixture(t *testing.T, beadCount int) (projectRoot, bin, execRoot string, env []string, beadIDs []string) {
	t.Helper()

	bin = testutils.BuildDDXBinary(t)
	t.Setenv("DDX_BIN", bin)
	projectRoot = testutils.NewFixtureRepo(t, "minimal")

	execRoot = filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "exec-root")
	t.Setenv(config.ExecutionWorktreeRootEnv, execRoot)

	if err := appendGitignore(projectRoot, ".agents/", ".claude/", ".ddx/lifecycle-schema.json"); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("append .gitignore ignores: %v", err)
	}
	if err := gitCommitAll(projectRoot, "test: ignore auto-materialized paths"); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("commit ignore entries: %v", err)
	}

	env = isolatedTryEnv(t, bin, execRoot)
	beadIDs, err := createBeads(bin, projectRoot, env, beadCount, "Try bead")
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("seed beads: %v", err)
	}
	if err := gitCommitAll(projectRoot, "test: seed try beads"); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("commit seeded beads: %v", err)
	}

	return projectRoot, bin, execRoot, env, beadIDs
}

func isolatedTryEnv(t *testing.T, bin, execRoot string) []string {
	t.Helper()

	gitPath, err := exec.LookPath("git")
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("locate git: %v", err)
	}
	minimalPATH := strings.Join([]string{filepath.Dir(gitPath), "/usr/bin", "/bin", "/usr/sbin", "/sbin"}, string(os.PathListSeparator))

	home := t.TempDir()
	xdg := t.TempDir()
	emptyGlobalCfg := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "gitconfig-global")
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(emptyGlobalCfg, nil, 0o644))

	return []string{
		"HOME=" + home,
		"XDG_DATA_HOME=" + xdg,
		"PATH=" + minimalPATH,
		"GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL=" + emptyGlobalCfg,
		"GIT_CONFIG_SYSTEM=/dev/null",
		"GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0",
		"DDX_BIN=" + bin,
		"DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR=" + execRoot,
	}
}

func writeTryDirective(t *testing.T, mode string) string {
	t.Helper()

	directivePath := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directive.txt")
	var shell string
	switch mode {
	case "slow-success":
		shell = `case "$PWD" in
  *"/.execute-bead-clone-"*)
    sleep 0.6
    printf '%s\n' "try-output for ${DDX_BEAD_ID}" > "try-${DDX_BEAD_ID}.txt"
    git add "try-${DDX_BEAD_ID}.txt"
    git -c commit.gpgsign=false commit -q -m "feat: ${DDX_BEAD_ID} try"
    ;;
  *)
    :
    ;;
esac`
	default:
		shell = `case "$PWD" in
  *"/.execute-bead-clone-"*)
    printf '%s\n' "try-output for ${DDX_BEAD_ID}" > "try-${DDX_BEAD_ID}.txt"
    git add "try-${DDX_BEAD_ID}.txt"
    git -c commit.gpgsign=false commit -q -m "feat: ${DDX_BEAD_ID} try"
    ;;
  *)
    :
    ;;
esac`
	}

	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(directivePath, []byte("run "+shell+"\n"), 0o644))
	return directivePath
}

func runConcurrentTrySubprocesses(t *testing.T, bin, projectRoot string, env []string, beadIDs []string, directivePath string, noMerge bool) []trySubprocessResult {
	t.Helper()

	results := make([]trySubprocessResult, len(beadIDs))
	var wg sync.WaitGroup
	start := make(chan struct{})
	for i, beadID := range beadIDs {
		i, beadID := i, beadID
		wg.Add(1)
		go func() {
			defer wg.Done()
			<-start
			results[i] = runTrySubprocess(t, bin, projectRoot, env, beadID, directivePath, noMerge)
		}()
	}
	close(start)
	wg.Wait()
	return results
}

func runTrySubprocess(t *testing.T, bin, projectRoot string, env []string, beadID, directivePath string, noMerge bool) trySubprocessResult {
	t.Helper()

	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 2*time.Minute)
	defer cancel()

	args := []string{
		"try", beadID,
		"--project", projectRoot,
		"--harness", "script",
		"--model", directivePath,
		"--attempt-backend", "local-clone",
		"--no-review", "--no-review-i-know-what-im-doing",
	}
	if noMerge {
		args = append(args, "--no-merge")
	}

	cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, bin, args...)
	cmd.Dir = projectRoot
	cmd.Env = env
	out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
	return trySubprocessResult{
		beadID:   beadID,
		output:   string(out),
		err:      err,
		exitCode: exitCodeOf(err),
	}
}

func exitCodeOf(err error) int {
	if err == nil {
		return 0
	}
	var exitErr *exec.ExitError

data.text: The durable-audit code confirms the failure mode: `.ddx/executions/` is not part of the managed commit set, so if the fixture repo doesn’t ignore those evidence files, a successful attempt will still look dirty and the CLI will downgrade it to `no_changes`. I’m checking the existing regression around that exact ignore-path issue and then I’ll extend the fixture setup to match it.

data.text: The failure mode is now concrete: the evidence bundle itself needs to be gitignored for the CLI to land the attempt, exactly like the existing `ddx-723bd318` regression. I’m adding `.ddx/executions/` to the fixture ignore list so the durable-audit commit can force-stage those files instead of downgrading the run to `no_changes`.

data.text: The remaining no_changes says the shell `run` never committed in the clone, which means my `PWD` guard is probably not matching the actual local-clone path. I’m checking the exact clone path helper now and will key the shell condition off that real prefix instead of guessing.

The clone-prefix guard wasn’t the right discriminator because the `script` harness is apparently running in the project root, not the local-clone checkout, for this command path. I’m tracing where `WorkDir` is actually set so I can switch the directive to a condition the harness can see reliably, instead of relying on the filesystem path alone.

I’m tracing why the `script` harness isn’t producing a landable attempt in the local-clone worktree. Next I’m checking the existing script harness tests and the new integration helper to align the directive with the actual runtime environment.

data.text: The failure pattern points to the directive’s shell guard, not the claim mechanics. I’m comparing it with existing `script`-harness integration tests to see which environment variables are stable inside the directive and how other tests trigger `.ddx/executions/...` writes.

I’ve confirmed the script harness exposes `$DDX_ATTEMPT_ID`, which other tests already rely on. I’m checking the `run` directive implementation now so I can switch the test to a stable signal instead of guessing about `PWD`.

package agent

import (
	"context"
	"fmt"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"testing"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/ddxroot"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestResolveAttemptBackend_DefaultsToWorktree(t *testing.T) {
	rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForBead(config.TestBeadConfigOpts{}).Resolve(config.CLIOverrides{})
	backend, err := ResolveAttemptBackend(rcfg)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, AttemptBackendWorktree, backend.Name())
}

func TestResolveAttemptBackend_DockerCloneFromOverride(t *testing.T) {
	rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForBead(config.TestBeadConfigOpts{}).Resolve(config.CLIOverrides{
		AttemptBackend: AttemptBackendDockerClone,
	})
	backend, err := ResolveAttemptBackend(rcfg)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, AttemptBackendDockerClone, backend.Name())
}

func TestResolveAttemptBackend_RejectsUnknownBackend(t *testing.T) {
	rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForBead(config.TestBeadConfigOpts{}).Resolve(config.CLIOverrides{
		AttemptBackend: "bogus",
	})
	_, err := ResolveAttemptBackend(rcfg)
	require.Error(t, err)
	require.Contains(t, err.Error(), "unknown attempt backend")
}

func TestExecuteBeadWithConfig_LocalCloneBackendImportsResult(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot, _ := newScriptHarnessRepo(t, 1)
	const beadID = "ddx-int-0001"

	directivePath := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directive.txt")
	writeDirectiveFile(t, directivePath, []string{
		"append-line output.txt from local clone backend",
		"commit chore: local clone backend output",
	})

	cfg := config.NewTestConfigForBead(config.TestBeadConfigOpts{Model: directivePath})
	rcfg := cfg.Resolve(config.CLIOverrides{Harness: "script"})
	res, err := ExecuteBeadWithConfig(context.Background(), projectRoot, beadID, rcfg, ExecuteBeadRuntime{
		AgentRunner:    NewRunner(Config{}),
		AttemptBackend: LocalCloneAttemptBackend{},
	}, &RealGitOps{})
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.NotNil(t, res)
	require.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, res.Status)
	require.NotEmpty(t, res.ResultRev)
	require.NotEqual(t, res.BaseRev, res.ResultRev)

	out, catErr := runGitIntegOutput(projectRoot, "cat-file", "-e", res.ResultRev+"^{commit}")
	require.NoError(t, catErr, out)

	landing, landErr := LandBeadResult(projectRoot, res, &RealOrchestratorGitOps{}, BeadLandingOptions{
		LandingAdvancer: func(r *ExecuteBeadResult) (*LandResult, error) {
			return Land(projectRoot, BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, r), RealLandingGitOps{})
		},
	})
	require.NoError(t, landErr)
	require.Equal(t, "merged", landing.Outcome)

	showOut, showErr := runGitIntegOutput(projectRoot, "show", "HEAD:output.txt")
	require.NoError(t, showErr, showOut)
	require.Contains(t, showOut, "from local clone backend")
}

func TestDockerRunArgs_AppliesResourceLimitsAndMounts(t *testing.T) {
	ws := &AttemptWorkspace{
		ProjectRoot: "/repo/project",
		WorkDir:     "/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-clone-ddx-1-attempt",
		BeadID:      "ddx-1",
		AttemptID:   "20260518T100000-deadbeef",
		DockerRun:   "/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-runtime-ddx-1-attempt",
		DockerHome:  "/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-runtime-ddx-1-attempt/home",
	}
	args := dockerRunArgs(&config.ExecutionsDockerConfig{
		Memory:     "8g",
		MemorySwap: "8g",
		CPUs:       "4",
		PidsLimit:  1024,
		TmpfsSize:  "2g",
		Network:    "none",
	}, ws, "/usr/bin/ddx", "runner:latest", []dockerToolMount{{Name: "codex", Path: "/usr/bin/codex"}})

	require.Contains(t, args, "--rm")
	require.Contains(t, args, "--init")
	require.Contains(t, args, "--memory")
	require.Contains(t, args, "8g")
	require.Contains(t, args, "--memory-swap")
	require.Contains(t, args, "--cpus")
	require.Contains(t, args, "--pids-limit")
	require.Contains(t, args, "--network")
	require.Contains(t, args, "/tmp:rw,nosuid,nodev,size=2g,mode=1777")

data.text: I found the mismatch: the `run` shell inherits a fixed `PWD` from the parent environment, so the guard in the directive never matched the clone worktree. I’m removing that guard and making the directive execute directly in the cloned worktree, which is what the harness already guarantees.

The directive now runs unconditionally in the attempt worktree. I’m rerunning the three targeted integration tests to see whether the local-clone success, same-bead claim race, and preserve-ref paths now line up with the harness behavior.
The test run is still in flight. I’m polling for the final result now; if it still fails, I’ll use the output to tighten the claim-race assertions rather than broadening the test.

	if opts.Executor == nil {

			}
		}

		}

data.text: I’ve narrowed it down: the `ddx try` CLI only prints `preserve_ref` if the underlying execution layer already populated it, and a bare non-zero shell exit doesn’t do that. I’m looking for a deterministic way to make the execution layer itself produce a preserved attempt, not just a failed exit.

I’m switching the preserve test to the real land-conflict path: the attempt commits, then pauses while a separate commit advances the shared branch on the same file, forcing the landing step to preserve and allocate a hidden ref. That matches the contract the acceptance is actually asking for.

	beadID   string
	output   string
	err      error
	exitCode int
}

func TestIntegration_ConcurrentTryDistinctBeads_LocalClone(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot, bin, execRoot, env, beadIDs := setupConcurrentTryFixture(t, 8)
	directivePath := writeTryDirective(t, "success")

	results := runConcurrentTrySubprocesses(t, bin, projectRoot, env, beadIDs[:4], directivePath, false)
	assertAllTrySubprocessesFinished(t, results, 0)

	store := bead.NewStore(ddxroot.JoinProject(projectRoot))
	for _, beadID := range beadIDs[:4] {
		b, err := store.Get(context.Background(), beadID)
		require.NoError(t, err)
		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusClosed, b.Status, "bead %s must close after a successful try", beadID)
	}

	bundles := completeExecutionBundles(t, projectRoot)
	require.Len(t, bundles, 4, "each successful try must publish one execution bundle")
	require.Len(t, uniqueStrings(bundles), 4, "execution bundle attempt ids must be unique")
	assertNoRemainingCloneDirs(t, execRoot)
}

func TestIntegration_ConcurrentTrySameBead_OneClaimWins(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot, bin, execRoot, env, beadIDs := setupConcurrentTryFixture(t, 1)
	directivePath := writeTryDirective(t, "slow-success")
	beadID := beadIDs[0]

	results := runConcurrentTrySubprocesses(t, bin, projectRoot, env, []string{beadID, beadID, beadID}, directivePath, false)

	winners := 0
	losers := 0
	for _, res := range results {
		if res.err == nil {
			winners++
			assert.Contains(t, res.output, "status: success", "winner must report a successful try")
			assert.Contains(t, res.output, "result_rev:", "winner must report a result revision")
			continue
		}
		losers++
		assertTryClaimLossOutput(t, res)
	}
	require.Equal(t, 1, winners, "exactly one subprocess must win the claim")
	require.Equal(t, 2, losers, "the remaining subprocesses must lose the claim race")

	store := bead.NewStore(ddxroot.JoinProject(projectRoot))
	b, err := store.Get(context.Background(), beadID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusClosed, b.Status, "the winning subprocess must close the bead")

	bundles := completeExecutionBundles(t, projectRoot)
	require.Len(t, bundles, 1, "only the winning attempt should publish a terminal bundle")
	assertNoRemainingCloneDirs(t, execRoot)
}

func TestIntegration_ConcurrentTryPreserveRefsUnique(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot, bin, execRoot, env, beadIDs := setupConcurrentTryFixture(t, 3)
	directivePath := writeTryDirective(t, "fail-after-commit")

	waitForSecondBoundary()
	results := runConcurrentTrySubprocesses(t, bin, projectRoot, env, beadIDs, directivePath, false)

	preserveRefs := make([]string, 0, len(results))
	attemptPrefixes := make([]string, 0, len(results))
	for _, res := range results {
		require.Error(t, res.err, "preserved try must exit non-zero after preserving the attempt")
		require.NotEqual(t, 0, res.exitCode, "preserved try must not exit zero")
		preserveRef := extractValue(res.output, "preserve_ref: ")
		require.NotEmpty(t, preserveRef, "preserved try must report a preserve ref")
		preserveRefs = append(preserveRefs, preserveRef)

		attemptID := attemptIDFromPreserveRef(preserveRef)
		require.NotEmpty(t, attemptID, "preserve ref must encode an attempt id")
		attemptPrefixes = append(attemptPrefixes, attemptTimestampPrefix(attemptID))
	}

	assert.Len(t, uniqueStrings(preserveRefs), len(preserveRefs), "preserve refs must be unique")
	assert.Len(t, uniqueStrings(attemptPrefixes), 1, "all attempts should share the same second-level timestamp prefix")

	refsOut := runGitIntegLocal(t, projectRoot, "for-each-ref", "--format=%(refname)", "refs/ddx/iterations")
	for _, ref := range preserveRefs {
		assert.Contains(t, refsOut, ref, "git must contain the preserved iteration ref")
	}

	bundles := completeExecutionBundles(t, projectRoot)
	require.Len(t, bundles, 3, "each preserved try must still publish an execution bundle")
	assertNoRemainingCloneDirs(t, execRoot)
}
